calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for (petitioner) Miyako Tanaka. Mark Brandenberg (son) also present.

The Court stated that the petition addresses a few pieces of real property, that the property held in joint tenancy would pass automatically, that it seems what petitioner is seeking is quiet title regarding the confusion on names, that the property held as Tenants in Common (TIC) presents a different issue as form of title does not automatically tell us who gets it, that there is evidence that it was purchases with community funds, that the Court is inclined to grant the petition as to the property held as TIC and that it is not inclined to act in respect to assets that pass as joint tenancy. Mr. Connors reported that their approach was for judicial economy, that it did not seem that the statutes prohibit the request they are asking for, that there have been no objections, that the issue is of the name on the title of one of the deeds and that there is no downside to the order. The Court stated that legal and statutory procedures matter and that it matters to the court to proceed properly. Mr. Connors stated that there is no question that the property was purchased with joint funds, explained how the property would pass, that in the context of the situation the petitioner felt compelled to use her husband's name although she never legally changed her name, that they should be able to accomplish what is necessary to clean up the title and that they anticipate a problem with the name when the property is sold. The Court pointed out that the problem may or may not arise. Mr. Connors stated that they needed to bring the spousal property petition regarding the TIC so they presented all of it. Mr. Brandenberg stated that his mother has dementia which is why she is not present and tried to ask questions of the court. The Court directed Mr. Brandenberg to speak to an attorney for legal advice. The petition for spousal property was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Continued To Nov-17-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
